page_title: "datahub_recipe_dynamodb_document Data Source - datahub"

description: |-
  Builds a DataHub ingestion recipe JSON document for the datahub_ingest resource.
  You provide structured inputs and get back a JSON string.
  Example Usage
  
  data "datahub_recipe_dynamodb_document" "example" {
    # source_id is optional; if omitted it is computed by hashing from source_type + source_config
    # source_id = "example-dynamodb-source"
    # source_type is optional and defaults to "dynamodb"
  
    # pipeline_name defaults to "<source_type>:<source_id>"
    # pipeline_name = "dynamodb:example-dynamodb-source"
  
    # NOTE: Prefer DataHub Secrets / env vars instead of raw credentials.
    source_config = {
      # Credentials are optional; if omitted the connector can use AWS default credential discovery
      # (env vars, profile, IAM role, etc).
      # aws_access_key_id     = "$${AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID}"
      # aws_secret_access_key = "$${AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY}"
      aws_region            = "<my-aws-region>"
      env                   = "STG"
  
      # Optional patterns
      database_pattern = {
        allow = ["<my-aws-region>.<my-database>.*"]
      }
  
      table_pattern = {
        allow = ["<my-aws-region>.<my-database>.<my-table>.*"]
      }
  
      domain = {
        my_datahub_domain = {
          allow = [".*"]
        }
      }
  
      stateful_ingestion = {
        enabled             = true
        fail_safe_threshold = 85
      }
  
      # Optional capabilities
      extract_table_tags = false
    }
  }
  
  resource "datahub_ingest" "example" {
    source_id   = "example-dynamodb-source"
    source_name = "Example DynamoDB Source"
    recipe = data.datahub_recipe_dynamodb_document.example.json
  }
  
  Argument Reference
  source_id (Optional) Ingestion source identifier. Used to default pipeline_name when omitted and commonly reused as datahub_ingest.source_id. If omitted, the provider computes a stable value by hashing source_type + source_config and returns it in state.source_type (Optional) Must be dynamodb. If omitted, defaults to dynamodb.source_config (Required) A map/object that is embedded verbatim under source.config in the recipe JSON. The provider does not validate fields; it simply serializes the object.pipeline_name (Optional) Defaults to <source_type>:<source_id>.
  Notes
  The upstream connector requires aws_region (breaking change starting in DataHub ingestion v0.13.3).
  DynamoDB recipe options: https://docs.datahub.com/docs/generated/ingestion/sources/dynamodb

# datahub_recipe_dynamodb_document (Data Source)

Builds a DataHub ingestion recipe JSON document for the `datahub_ingest` resource.

You provide structured inputs and get back a JSON string.

## Example Usage

```terraform
data "datahub_recipe_dynamodb_document" "example" {
  # source_id is optional; if omitted it is computed by hashing from source_type + source_config
  # source_id = "example-dynamodb-source"
  # source_type is optional and defaults to "dynamodb"

  # pipeline_name defaults to "<source_type>:<source_id>"
  # pipeline_name = "dynamodb:example-dynamodb-source"

  # NOTE: Prefer DataHub Secrets / env vars instead of raw credentials.
  source_config = {
    # Credentials are optional; if omitted the connector can use AWS default credential discovery
    # (env vars, profile, IAM role, etc).
    # aws_access_key_id     = "$${AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID}"
    # aws_secret_access_key = "$${AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY}"
    aws_region            = "<my-aws-region>"
    env                   = "STG"

    # Optional patterns
    database_pattern = {
      allow = ["<my-aws-region>.<my-database>.*"]
    }

    table_pattern = {
      allow = ["<my-aws-region>.<my-database>.<my-table>.*"]
    }

    domain = {
      my_datahub_domain = {
        allow = [".*"]
      }
    }

    stateful_ingestion = {
      enabled             = true
      fail_safe_threshold = 85
    }

    # Optional capabilities
    extract_table_tags = false
  }
}

resource "datahub_ingest" "example" {
  source_id   = "example-dynamodb-source"
  source_name = "Example DynamoDB Source"
  recipe = data.datahub_recipe_dynamodb_document.example.json
}
```

## Argument Reference

- `source_id` (Optional) Ingestion source identifier. Used to default `pipeline_name` when omitted and commonly reused as `datahub_ingest.source_id`. If omitted, the provider computes a stable value by hashing `source_type` + `source_config` and returns it in state.
- `source_type` (Optional) Must be `dynamodb`. If omitted, defaults to `dynamodb`.
- `source_config` (Required) A map/object that is embedded verbatim under `source.config` in the recipe JSON. The provider does not validate fields; it simply serializes the object.
- `pipeline_name` (Optional) Defaults to `<source_type>:<source_id>`.

## Notes

The upstream connector requires `aws_region` (breaking change starting in DataHub ingestion v0.13.3).

DynamoDB recipe options: https://docs.datahub.com/docs/generated/ingestion/sources/dynamodb



<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `source_config` (Dynamic) Arbitrary source config object embedded under `source.config`. This is serialized as-is into JSON (no field validation). For DynamoDB fields, see https://docs.datahub.com/docs/generated/ingestion/sources/dynamodb. AWS credentials are optional and can be omitted to use default AWS credential discovery. Prefer using `$${SECRET_NAME}` or `$${ENV_VAR}` placeholders for sensitive values.

### Optional

- `pipeline_name` (String) Optional pipeline name. If omitted, defaults to `<source_type>:<source_id>`.
- `source_id` (String) Ingestion source id. Used to default `pipeline_name` when not set and commonly reused as `datahub_ingest.source_id`. If omitted, the provider computes a stable value by hashing `source_type` + `source_config` and returns it in state.
- `source_type` (String) Recipe source type (the DataHub ingestion source type). For this data source it must be `dynamodb`. If omitted, defaults to `dynamodb`.

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) Stable identifier derived from the JSON output.
- `json` (String) Rendered recipe JSON. If you included secrets inline in `source_config`, they will appear here.
